tools.name: publish_content
tools.description:
Publish content to social media platforms.

MEDIA RULES:
• mediaUrl must be a public HTTPS URL — NOT a local file path.
• If the user shares an image/video in chat, call create_upload_session FIRST to get a browser upload link, then use the returned URL here.
• Text-only works on: LinkedIn, Threads,

tools.name: schedule_content
tools.description:
Schedule content for future publishing. Same media rules as publish_content apply.

• mediaUrl must be a public HTTPS URL — call create_upload_session if user shares a file in chat.
• Content is validated at schedule time, not publish time.

tools.name: get_analytics
tools.description: Get engagement analytics for a platform. Facebook and TikTok can include account-wide posts not published through SendIt; unresolved TikTok inbox-draft deliveries may appear as placeholders until SendIt can link them.

tools.name: list_teams
tools.description: List all teams you belong to. Returns team names, IDs, and your role. Use the returned team ID with the team_id parameter in other tools to operate in team context.

tools.name: list_connected_accounts
tools.description: List all connected social media accounts. Pass team_id to see a team's accounts instead of your personal ones.

tools.name: get_tiktok_creator_info
tools.description: Fetch fresh TikTok Content Posting API creator info for the connected account. Use this before rendering TikTok publish settings.

tools.name: get_scheduled_posts
tools.description: List scheduled posts and their current status, including pending, publishing, TikTok draft-delivered, published, failed, or cancelled posts.

tools.name: delete_scheduled_post
tools.description: Cancel a scheduled post before it's published.

tools.name: delete_post
tools.description:
Delete a published post from a platform. Supports: X, Facebook, LinkedIn, Threads, YouTube, Pinterest, Bluesky, Mastodon, Telegram.

Provide the post ID (database ID returned when published through SendIt), the platform post ID, or the post URL.

tools.name: edit_post
tools.description:
Edit a published post on a supported platform. Updates the text/caption of an already published post.

SUPPORTED: YouTube (title/description/tags), LinkedIn, Facebook, Mastodon, Telegram, Bluesky
NOT SUPPORTED: X, Threads, Instagram, Pinterest, TikTok

Requires the published post ID returned when th

tools.name: get_platform_requirements
tools.description:
Get detailed content requirements for a platform.

Returns: Character limits, media specifications, rate limits, and special notes.

Call this when you need specifics like exact character counts, file size limits, or supported formats. The publish_content description has a quick reference, but this

tools.name: validate_content
tools.description:
Validate content against platform requirements BEFORE publishing.

USE THIS WHEN:
• Unsure if content will work on target platforms
• Publishing to multiple platforms with different requirements
• Want to catch errors before attempting publish

Returns specific errors (e.g., 'TikTok requires video',

tools.name: upload_media
tools.description:
FOR CLAUDE DESKTOP ONLY (with filesystem access).
For Claude.ai/web: Use create_upload_session instead - it provides a browser upload link.

Upload local media to cloud storage, returning a public HTTPS URL.

WHEN TO USE:
• Instagram, LinkedIn, Threads, X: REQUIRED for local files before calling pub

tools.name: create_upload_session
tools.description:
Create a browser upload link for media files. ALWAYS use this when the user shares an image or video in chat — their file is local and cannot be passed directly to publish_content.

WORKFLOW:
1. Call this tool to get an uploadUrl
2. Give the user the link to open in their browser and upload their fi

tools.name: get_upload_session
tools.description:
Check the status of an upload session and get the media URL(s) once uploaded.

Call this after the user clicks the upload link to see if they've completed the upload.

Returns:
• status: 'pending' - User hasn't uploaded yet
• status: 'uploaded' - Upload complete, includes mediaUrl, mediaUrls, fileCo

tools.name: create_goal
tools.description:
Create a new maker goal on Product Hunt.

REQUIREMENTS:
• Must have Product Hunt account connected
• Write access requires app whitelisting by Product Hunt
• Goal title limited to 280 characters

If write access is not available, you'll receive an error with instructions to request whitelisting.

tools.name: complete_goal
tools.description:
Mark a Product Hunt goal as complete or incomplete.

REQUIREMENTS:
• Must have Product Hunt account connected
• Write access requires app whitelisting by Product Hunt

tools.name: upvote_product
tools.description:
Upvote a product on Product Hunt.

REQUIREMENTS:
• Must have Product Hunt account connected
• Write access requires app whitelisting by Product Hunt

Provide the Product Hunt post ID (not the slug).

tools.name: get_producthunt_analytics
tools.description:
Get analytics for your products on Product Hunt.

Returns:
• Total products, votes, comments, reviews
• Number of featured products
• Per-product metrics including vote counts and ratings

Works with read-only access (no whitelisting required).

tools.name: bulk_schedule
tools.description:
Schedule multiple posts at once from CSV content.

USE THIS WHEN:
• User has a spreadsheet or list of posts to schedule
• Planning a content calendar for a month
• Migrating content from another tool

CSV FORMAT (required columns):
• platform: linkedin, instagram, x, tiktok, threads
• scheduled_time

tools.name: preview_content
tools.description:
Generate a visual preview of how content will appear on each platform.

USE THIS WHEN:
• Before publishing to see how posts will look
• To validate content against platform requirements
• To check character counts, hashtag limits, and media requirements

Returns an HTML preview mockup for each platf

tools.name: connect_x
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your X account.

tools.name: connect_linkedin
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your LinkedIn account.

tools.name: connect_linkedin-page
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your LinkedIn Page account.

tools.name: connect_facebook
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your Facebook account.

tools.name: connect_instagram
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your Instagram account.

tools.name: connect_threads
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your Threads account.

tools.name: connect_bluesky
tools.description: Get the credential setup schema to connect Bluesky using API key or token credentials.

tools.name: connect_mastodon
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your Mastodon account.

tools.name: connect_nostr
tools.description: Get the custom connection setup instructions for Nostr. Note: This is an unofficial connector and may change without notice.

tools.name: connect_youtube
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your YouTube account.

tools.name: connect_tiktok
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your TikTok account.

tools.name: connect_lemmy
tools.description: Get the credential setup schema to connect Lemmy using API key or token credentials.

tools.name: connect_discord
tools.description: Get the webhook setup schema to connect Discord.

tools.name: connect_slack
tools.description: Get the webhook setup schema to connect Slack.

tools.name: connect_telegram
tools.description: Get the credential setup schema to connect Telegram using API key or token credentials.

tools.name: connect_pinterest
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your Pinterest account.

tools.name: connect_dribbble
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your Dribbble account.

tools.name: connect_devto
tools.description: Get the credential setup schema to connect DEV.to using API key or token credentials.

tools.name: connect_hashnode
tools.description: Get the credential setup schema to connect Hashnode using API key or token credentials.

tools.name: connect_gmb
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your Google My Business account.

tools.name: connect_whop
tools.description: Get the credential setup schema to connect Whop using API key or token credentials. Note: This is an unofficial connector and may change without notice.

tools.name: connect_producthunt
tools.description: Get the OAuth URL to connect your Product Hunt account.

tools.name: list_library
tools.description: List saved content from your library. Returns drafts, templates, and evergreen content.

tools.name: get_library_item
tools.description: Get a specific content library item by ID.

tools.name: save_to_library
tools.description: Save content to your library as a draft, template, or evergreen content for reuse.

tools.name: update_library_item
tools.description: Update an existing content library item.

tools.name: delete_library_item
tools.description: Delete a content library item.

tools.name: publish_from_library
tools.description: Publish content directly from your library to one or more platforms.

tools.name: trigger_scheduled_post
tools.description: Manually trigger a scheduled post to publish immediately.

tools.name: list_dead_letter
tools.description: List posts that failed all retry attempts and were moved to the dead letter queue. Review and decide to requeue or discard them.

tools.name: requeue_dead_letter
tools.description: Requeue a dead letter post for another publishing attempt. Creates a new scheduled post from the dead letter entry.

tools.name: list_brand_voices
tools.description: List all brand voice profiles. Brand voices guide AI content generation with tone, personality, writing rules, and example posts.

tools.name: create_brand_voice
tools.description:
Create a brand voice profile to guide AI content generation.

Configurable fields:
• tone: Overall tone (professional, casual, energetic, witty, etc.)
• personality: Brand personality description
• writingStyle: Writing style guidelines
• doRules: Array of things the brand voice SHOULD do
• dontRule

tools.name: set_default_brand_voice
tools.description: Set a brand voice profile as the default. The default brand voice is automatically used by all AI content generation tools.

tools.name: list_pending_approvals
tools.description: List all scheduled posts that are pending approval. These posts won't be published until approved.

tools.name: approve_post
tools.description: Approve a scheduled post that is pending approval. Once approved, it will be published at the scheduled time.

tools.name: reject_post
tools.description: Reject a scheduled post that is pending approval. The post will not be published.

tools.name: list_inbox
tools.description:
List comment threads on your published posts.

Returns threads from your social inbox, showing comments and replies from followers on your posts across all platforms.

tools.name: reply_to_comment
tools.description:
Reply to a comment on one of your published posts.

Supported platforms: X (reply tweets), Threads, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube.
Not supported: LinkedIn, Pinterest, TikTok.

tools.name: get_best_times
tools.description:
Get the optimal posting times for a platform based on your historical engagement data.

Returns top time slots ranked by engagement score. Falls back to industry defaults when insufficient personal data exists.

tools.name: score_content
tools.description:
Score content quality on a 0-100 scale before publishing.

Evaluates 5 factors (20 points each):
1. Text length optimization for target platforms
2. Hashtag count optimization
3. Posting time alignment with best engagement windows
4. Media presence (images/videos)
5. Content patterns (CTA, hooks, fo

tools.name: get_audit_log
tools.description:
Retrieve audit log entries for your account.

Shows a timestamped trail of all actions performed: publishes, schedules, account connections, content library changes, etc.

Filter by action type, resource type, or date range.

tools.name: generate_ai_media
tools.description:
Generate AI images or videos using approved media providers.

Supported providers:
- heygen-mcp: HeyGen Direct API or MCP video/avatar generation
- codex-oauth-image: Codex OAuth image generation for gpt-image-2

Returns a job ID that can be polled for status.

tools.name: check_ai_media_status
tools.description:
Check the status of an AI media generation job.

Returns current status (pending, processing, completed, failed) and result URL when complete.

tools.name: generate_content
tools.description:
Generate AI-powered platform-optimized content without publishing.

Uses AI to create platform-specific text, hashtags, and titles from a prompt or media URL.
Respects brand voice profiles if configured.

Returns generated content variants for each target platform. Use publish_content to publish the

tools.name: publish_ai
tools.description:
Generate AI content and publish it in a single step.

Combines generate_content + publish_content into one call.
First generates platform-optimized content using AI, then publishes to the specified platforms.

Same platform requirements as publish_content apply.

tools.name: create_library_item
tools.description: Save content to your library as a draft, template, or evergreen content for reuse. Alias for save_to_library.

tools.name: draft_reply
tools.description: Generate an AI-powered reply draft for a social mention. Returns a draft with tone and safety flags.

tools.name: summarize_mentions
tools.description: Cluster and summarize recent social mentions by theme and sentiment using AI.

tools.name: generate_post_bundle
tools.description: Generate multi-variant AI content with quality scoring for multiple platforms. Returns the best variant plus alternatives.

tools.name: critique_post
tools.description: Score and critique post content with AI-powered suggestions. Returns a quality score (0-100), breakdown, and improvement tips.

tools.name: connect_platform
tools.description: Connect a platform dynamically by platform ID. Returns OAuth URL, webhook setup details, or credential form contract depending on the platform auth method.

tools.name: get_platform_settings_schema
tools.description: Get the JSON schema contract for content.platformSettings.<platformId>, including platform-specific publish options, validation-only gates, and supported per-platform content overrides.

tools.name: list_connectors
tools.description:
List all available connectors in the SendIt platform.

Returns connectors organized by category: organic, paid_media, automation, workspace.
Each connector includes its ID, name, status, auth strategy, and capabilities.

Use this to discover what integrations are available before connecting.

tools.name: get_connector_capabilities
tools.description:
Get detailed capabilities and operations for a specific connector.

Returns supported operations, auth requirements, rate limits, and constraints.

tools.name: connect_connector
tools.description:
Initiate authentication flow for a connector.

For OAuth2 connectors, returns an authorization URL.
For API key connectors, stores the provided key.
For webhook connectors, registers the webhook URL.

tools.name: disconnect_connector
tools.description: Disconnect and revoke credentials for a connector.

tools.name: list_connected_connectors
tools.description:
List all currently connected connectors with their status and health.
Includes both legacy platform connections and new connector connections.

tools.name: get_connector_health
tools.description: Get health status and SLO metrics for a connector.

tools.name: execute_connector_operation
tools.description:
Execute a specific operation on a connected connector.

Use get_connector_capabilities to discover available operations.
Operations include read/write actions specific to each connector.

tools.name: list_ad_accounts
tools.description:
List all connected advertising accounts across platforms.
Returns ad accounts from Meta Ads, Google Ads, LinkedIn Ads, TikTok Ads, etc.

tools.name: create_ad_campaign
tools.description:
Create a new advertising campaign on a connected ad platform.

Supports campaign creation across Meta Ads, Google Ads, LinkedIn Ads, TikTok Ads, Pinterest Ads, and all other connected ad platforms.

The AI Campaign Builder agent can help design optimal campaign structures.

tools.name: list_ad_campaigns
tools.description: List campaigns for an ad account with status and performance summary.

tools.name: update_ad_campaign
tools.description: Update campaign settings (name, budget, dates, status).

tools.name: create_ad_creative
tools.description:
Create an ad creative (image, video, carousel, or text ad).

The Creative Asset agent can help generate ad visuals and copy.

tools.name: get_ad_performance
tools.description:
Get performance metrics for campaigns, ad sets, or creatives.

Returns impressions, clicks, spend, conversions, CTR, CPC, CPM, ROAS, and custom metrics.
Supports date range filtering and breakdown by day.

tools.name: get_unified_ad_report
tools.description:
Get a cross-platform advertising performance report.

Aggregates metrics across all connected ad platforms into a single view.
Useful for comparing performance across Meta, Google, LinkedIn, TikTok, etc.

tools.name: repurpose_url
tools.description:
Takes a URL (article, YouTube video, or podcast) and repurposes its content into platform-optimized posts.

Supports:
• Web articles — extracts text content
• YouTube videos — extracts transcript/captions
• Podcast RSS feeds — extracts episode descriptions and show notes

Returns platform-specific f

tools.name: list_agents
tools.description:
List all available AI agents and their capabilities.

SendIt includes 12 specialized agents:
• Strategy Planner - Content strategy from audience/trend analysis
• Content Ideation - Topic ideas from trends and calendar gaps
• Multi-Format Composer - Platform-optimized content from a brief
• Creative

tools.name: invoke_agent
tools.description:
Invoke a specific AI agent with given inputs.

The agent will execute within policy constraints and return structured output.
All agent runs are logged for audit and traceability.

tools.name: get_agent_run
tools.description: Get the details and output of a specific agent run.

tools.name: list_agent_runs
tools.description: List recent agent runs with status and summary.

tools.name: get_agent_policies
tools.description: Get the active policies/guardrails for an agent.

tools.name: update_agent_policy
tools.description:
Create or update a policy/guardrail for an agent.

Policy types:
• brand_safety - Content must align with brand guidelines
• platform_compliance - Must follow platform ad policies
• approval_gate - Require human approval before execution
• budget_limit - Cap spending or resource usage
• content_filt

tools.name: list_workflows
tools.description: List all automation workflows with their status and trigger info.

tools.name: create_workflow
tools.description:
Create a new automation workflow.

Workflows consist of a trigger and a sequence of steps.

Trigger types:
• manual - Run on demand
• schedule - Cron-based schedule
• event - Triggered by SendIt events (post published, mention detected, etc.)
• webhook - Triggered by external webhook
• connector_eve

tools.name: update_workflow
tools.description: Update a workflow's configuration or status.

tools.name: delete_workflow
tools.description: Delete a workflow permanently.

tools.name: trigger_workflow
tools.description: Manually trigger a workflow run.

tools.name: list_workflow_runs
tools.description: List recent runs for a workflow.

tools.name: get_workflow_run
tools.description: Get detailed step-by-step execution log for a workflow run.

tools.name: get_unified_analytics
tools.description:
Get cross-platform analytics combining organic and paid performance.

Aggregates metrics across all connected platforms into a single view.
Supports time-series breakdowns and platform comparison.

tools.name: get_attribution_report
tools.description:
Generate an attribution report showing how channels contribute to conversions.

Models:
• first_touch - All credit to first interaction
• last_touch - All credit to last interaction
• linear - Equal credit across all touchpoints
• time_decay - More credit to recent touchpoints
• position_based - 40%

tools.name: get_anomaly_alerts
tools.description:
Get recent anomaly alerts detected across metrics.

Includes sudden drops/spikes in engagement, unusual spend patterns, and content performance outliers.

tools.name: get_benchmark_comparison
tools.description:
Compare your performance against industry benchmarks.

Returns how your engagement rates, growth, and content performance compare to similar accounts in your industry.

tools.name: get_post_analytics
tools.description: Fetch drilldown analytics for one published post, including per-snapshot metrics and campaign context.

tools.name: list_analytics_reports
tools.description: List saved analytics report definitions for the current personal or team scope.

tools.name: run_analytics_report
tools.description: Execute one saved analytics report immediately and return the generated run plus aggregated analytics data.

tools.name: create_scheduled_report
tools.description: Create a recurring analytics report that's generated and delivered automatically.

tools.name: list_conversations
tools.description:
List unified inbox conversations across all connected channels.

Aggregates comments, mentions, DMs, and messages from all organic and workspace connectors into a single inbox view.

tools.name: get_conversation
tools.description: Get full conversation thread with all messages.

tools.name: reply_to_conversation
tools.description:
Send a reply in a conversation.

The reply is sent through the original channel (e.g., Instagram comment, X reply, Facebook comment, etc.).

Replies require conversation reply routing metadata (credential + operation). Provide "routing" once and it will be stored on the conversation for future repli

tools.name: update_conversation
tools.description: Update conversation status, priority, assignment, or tags.

tools.name: get_inbox_summary
tools.description: Get a summary of the unified inbox: total conversations, unread count, sentiment breakdown, and response time metrics.

tools.name: escalate_to_support
tools.description:
Escalate a social conversation to a connected support platform (Zendesk, Intercom, etc.).

Creates a support ticket from the conversation context.

tools.name: autopilot_set_goal
tools.description:
Set a social media goal for the AI autopilot to work toward.

The autopilot will use AI agents to plan content, generate posts, and schedule them.
Example goals: 'Grow LinkedIn followers by 20%', 'Post 5x/week on Instagram about AI'.

tools.name: autopilot_generate_plan
tools.description:
Generate a weekly content plan for an autopilot goal.

Chains 5 AI agents: Strategy Planner -> Content Ideation -> Calendar Optimizer -> Multi-Format Composer -> Variant Repurposer.
Returns a plan with individual post items for review.

tools.name: autopilot_approve_plan
tools.description:
Approve all pending items in an autopilot plan, or approve/reject individual items.

After approval, use autopilot_execute_plan to schedule the approved posts.

tools.name: autopilot_execute_plan
tools.description:
Schedule all approved posts in an autopilot plan.

Posts are spaced out over time and scheduled via the standard publishing pipeline.

tools.name: autopilot_get_progress
tools.description: Get progress metrics for an autopilot goal — weeks planned, posts approved, scheduled, published.

tools.name: autopilot_list_goals
tools.description: List all autopilot goals for the current user.

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: List imported product/codebase projects for social strategy and content generation.

tools.name: create_project
tools.description: Create a Projects workspace for a product, app, or codebase.

tools.name: import_project_source
tools.description: Import GitHub, app-store, website, file, or media source context into a project.

tools.name: analyze_project
tools.description: Analyze a project for audience, positioning, differentiators, content pillars, and risks.

tools.name: get_project_analysis
tools.description: Fetch stored project analysis, strategy, recent generations, and activity.

tools.name: generate_project_strategy
tools.description: Generate project-local brand voice, positioning, audience, and social content strategy.

tools.name: request_project_content
tools.description: Generate UGC, slideshow, remix, caption, or calendar content from project intelligence.

tools.name: save_project_content_to_library
tools.description: Generate project content and save the outputs into the reusable content library.

tools.name: schedule_content_advanced
tools.description: Schedule content with explicit target accounts, queue placement, recurrence, platform overrides, media library attachments, and score gating.

tools.name: get_scheduled_post
tools.description: Fetch one scheduled post with approval, recurrence, retry, and published-link metadata.

tools.name: edit_scheduled_post
tools.description: Edit a pending scheduled post. Supports text, media, platform, timing, approval-scope, and publish-now updates.

tools.name: retry_scheduled_post
tools.description: Retry a failed scheduled post, optionally editing the content, narrowing to failed platforms, rescheduling, or publishing immediately.

tools.name: duplicate_scheduled_post
tools.description: Create a new scheduled post by duplicating an existing scheduled post. If no time is supplied, SendIt shifts it forward automatically.

tools.name: suggest_next_schedule_time
tools.description: Suggest the next recommended publish time using best-times insights or configured queue slots without creating a post.

tools.name: suggest_queue_slots
tools.description: Suggest reusable queue slots derived from best-time insights for one or more platforms.

tools.name: schedule_multilang_content
tools.description: Schedule the same post in multiple languages, linking all variants into one translation group.

tools.name: list_calendar_events
tools.description: List scheduled and published calendar events with optional date-range, status, platform, and pagination filters.

tools.name: update_calendar_event
tools.description: Update one calendar event by rescheduling it, changing status, reassigning it, or storing metadata.

tools.name: bulk_update_calendar_events
tools.description: Apply one bulk action to many calendar events at once, including shifts, explicit reschedules, queue moves, cancellations, or assignments.

tools.name: list_recurring_series
tools.description: List recurring scheduled-post series and their recurrence metadata.

tools.name: update_recurring_series
tools.description: Update the recurrence rule or end date for future occurrences in a recurring series.

tools.name: cancel_recurring_series
tools.description: Cancel a recurring series and stop future pending occurrences from publishing.

tools.name: get_calendar_gaps
tools.description: Detect open days in the publishing calendar and return gap metadata for planning.

tools.name: get_calendar_recommendations
tools.description: Return top recommended recurring posting slots from historical performance data.

tools.name: get_approval_comments
tools.description: List approval comments for one scheduled post in team approval scope.

tools.name: list_media_assets
tools.description: List media library assets with filtering by collection, type, tags, search, and pagination.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "upload_media" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: FOR CLAUDE DESKTOP ONLY (with filesystem access). For Claude.ai/web: Use create_upload_session instead - it provides a browser upload link. Upload local media t…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "bulk_schedule" is 692 chars, far longer than this server's median (110). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Schedule multiple posts at once from CSV content. USE THIS WHEN: • User has a spreadsheet or list of posts to schedule • Planning a content calendar for a month…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "connect_bluesky" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get the credential setup schema to connect Bluesky using API key or token credentials.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "connect_lemmy" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get the credential setup schema to connect Lemmy using API key or token credentials.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "connect_telegram" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get the credential setup schema to connect Telegram using API key or token credentials.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "connect_devto" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get the credential setup schema to connect DEV.to using API key or token credentials.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "connect_hashnode" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get the credential setup schema to connect Hashnode using API key or token credentials.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "connect_whop" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get the credential setup schema to connect Whop using API key or token credentials. Note: This is an unofficial connector and may change without notice.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "connect_connector" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Initiate authentication flow for a connector. For OAuth2 connectors, returns an authorization URL. For API key connectors, stores the provided key. For webhook …

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "disconnect_connector" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Disconnect and revoke credentials for a connector.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "list_agents" is 800 chars, far longer than this server's median (110). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: List all available AI agents and their capabilities. SendIt includes 12 specialized agents: • Strategy Planner - Content strategy from audience/trend analysis •…

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "create_workflow" is 715 chars, far longer than this server's median (110). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Create a new automation workflow. Workflows consist of a trigger and a sequence of steps. Trigger types: • manual - Run on demand • schedule - Cron-based schedu…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow.
